purpose: Static, machine-readable contract for Claude Code agents, hooks and commands. Runtime/generated state stays outside .claude.

static_config.policy: Agents must not edit static orchestrator config during app-building runs.

source_of_truth.runtime_policy: Do not edit source-of-truth while a TASK_ID is active. Change docs first, then bootstrap again.
source_of_truth.increment_policy: The five source-of-truth docs are cumulative. Keep existing baseline/built rows plus v1/v2/vN rows together; use Product increment and Build state in the Coverage Registry.

dag_scope.policy: DAG-only. Worker identity comes exclusively from CLAUDE_ACTIVE_TASK_ID + CLAUDE_TASK_PACK. No implicit task/phase selector exists.

derived_core_state.policy: Only bootstrap/claim/hooks/checker scripts may write these files directly. DAG-only mode stores no global task/implicit selector.
derived_core_state.canonical_dag_source: orchestrator-state/tasks/registry.json -> tasks[] + task_dag.source_digest; task-dag.json/md and execution-graph.json are derived views checked for drift by check-task-dag --strict

must_not_write: registry/runtime JSON directly unless explicit repair requested

may_write: shared config files explicitly declared in the DAG task Write set (e.g. docker-compose.yml, Dockerfile*, .env.example, .github/workflows/**)

must_not_write: product code except explicit fixture/test-only request

may_write: smallest product-code fix inside TASK_ID scope
git commit in configured workflow checkout
docs/product-baseline/* via sync_product_baseline.py after verified handoff only
docs/product-baseline/BASELINE_MANIFEST.json via sync_product_baseline.py after verified handoff only
must_not_write: mark done without push + cleanup proof
mark done with unrecorded out-of-scope findings; formal proposed follow-ups from this TASK_ID must be included in the PR, not used to block close
Co-authored-by trailers or Claude coauthor metadata in commits

may_write: deployment manifests/config/scripts inside task scope when deployment is explicitly in scope
must_not_write: deploy to a real target without approved QA/closer gate, dry-run evidence and rollback plan

may_write: docs/source-of-truth before execution starts when user-approved planning changes are required
must_not_write: registry/runtime/task-dag/work-items directly; bootstrap scripts write generated artifacts under locks

may_write: orchestration notes in handoffs only through the scoped agent/command workflow

must_not_write: register_followup_task.py propose; only the main-orchestrator or /verify-slice registers FU after reading followup_candidate=yes
no follow-up promotion or proposal; report followup_candidate in handoff only

slice-verifier.notes: Human-real verify gate. Writes handoff/evidence only; SubagentStop hook moves lifecycle to verified_pending_close/needs_debug/blocked.

ux_contract.source_docs: User journeys live in instrucciones.md; route/page/endpoints/UI states live in TECHNICAL_GUIDE; slice acceptance/verify lives in CHECKLIST.

rules: Use real/provided sandbox data for productive closure.
Do not insert verification data through the endpoint under test.
Synthetic data is allowed only for labelled edge cases such as empty/error/permission states.
Evidence must state data-contract rows used, real/provided data loaded and persisted data observed.

followup_tasks.purpose: Only real out-of-scope work becomes formal DAG follow-up. In-scope defects stay in the same TASK_ID and go through validator/tester -> debugger -> retest.
followup_tasks.policy: Agents may propose follow-ups. The origin closer must include formal proposed follow-up YAMLs in the slice PR and continue the close automatically; only later waves/claims are blocked until main-orchestrator promotes or waives them. Promotion persists the task into source-of-truth so future bootstrap runs do not lose it.
followup_tasks.promotion_policy: Use /promote-followup as the safe main-orchestrator command. The script is the low-level implementation and must not be called by closer automatically. The closer may carry proposed FU YAMLs in its PR, but must not convert them into DAG tasks. Main-orchestrator must reject/waive FU spam that should have gone through debugger/retest.
followup_tasks.triage_policy: Do not create FU for in_scope_defect. Use debugger/retest inside the current slice. Every FU must explain why debugger cannot fix it inside TASK_ID; blocking severities require --why-not-debugger.
followup_tasks.closer_policy: Formal proposed follow-ups from the same origin_task_id do not block closer/PR. They must be referenced in the report and staged by scripts/git-add-slice.sh; /next-wave and claim_task.py remain blocked until promote or waiver.
learned_triage_rules: out-of-write_set sibling bug => missing_coverage/out_of_scope, not in_scope_defect
duplicate FU => recommend waiver duplicate_of_done; subagents do not promote or waive
promoted FU write_set path drift => planner resolves real files with find/grep; do not open FU for string drift
promoted FU missing checklist row => document runtime_followup linkage in task pack; do not edit source-of-truth mid-slice

product_baseline.purpose: docs/product-baseline/ stores the built cumulative baseline snapshot passed to ChatGPT before planning the next increment.
product_baseline.policy: Only closer syncs the verified five-file baseline: docs/source-of-truth is copied into docs/product-baseline only after /verify-slice wrote VERIFY_OUTCOME=verified and validator/tester handoff sections are approved/pass. sync_product_baseline.py enforces this unless --allow-unverified is used for explicit orchestrator maintenance. Do not hand-edit baseline snapshot during active DAG tasks.

build_state_values.done: already built baseline, do not rebuild after bootstrap
build_state_values.ready: force ready root when dependencies allow

verify_policy.auto_verify_requires: low risk, deterministic commands, no closing journey, real/provided data evidence
verify_policy.state_machine: tester pass -> ready_for_close; slice-verifier verified -> verified_pending_close; closer committed -> done; issues_found -> needs_debug; blocked stays blocked until the responsible lifecycle agent reruns.
verify_policy.browser_mcp_isolation_policy: chrome-devtools is primary for every /verify-slice and must use --isolated or per-TASK_ID --browser-url/profile. claude-in-chrome is second fallback if chrome-devtools is not usable or cannot complete the required human session. agent360/browser-mcp is third fallback and may be used when the first two are unusable or the user/project explicitly requires Agent360 for cookies/MFA/session handling.
verify_policy.flutter_mobile_mcp_policy: Flutter mobile verification uses Dart/Flutter MCP (MCP_CLIENT dart|flutter|flutter-driver) with VISUAL_CHECK_METHOD simulator|emulator|device. Browser MCP remains valid for Flutter web only.
verify_policy.mobile_mcp_policy: Flutter/Dart mobile verification must use Dart/Flutter MCP via MCP_CLIENT (dart, flutter, flutter-driver) with VISUAL_CHECK_METHOD simulator/emulator/device; browser MCP remains valid for web/browser verification only.

trailer_schema.policy: All agents and command emitters must emit trailers using the role-specific enums below. Hooks validate against this schema only; if the schema is unavailable or a role is missing, hooks log a visible error and refuse lifecycle mutation. The slice-verifier role may move a task only to verified_pending_close/needs_debug/blocked; closer is the only actor allowed to move a task to done. Command-only roles such as verify-journey may emit JOURNEY_* state without a .claude/agents prompt file.

closer.description: Final closure: report, baseline sync, commit, push, runtime cleanup and worktree cleanup.
closer.transport_completion_policy: Closer NEXT_STATUS=done requires REPORT_READY, BASELINE_SYNC_READY, GIT_READY, PUSH_READY, GIT_WORKFLOW_READY, RUNTIME_CLEANED and WORKTREES_CLEANED all yes. For pr-flow it also requires PR_READY=yes, MERGED=yes and CANONICAL_MAIN_SYNCED=yes; PR open/queued/auto-merge pending is blocked, not done.

debugger.description: Applies the smallest fix inside the same TASK_ID after tester/validator failure.

deployer.description: Deployment planning/execution when the project enables deploy flow. Deployer never closes a task as done; it can leave deployment evidence ready for closer or block.

developer.description: Implements code for exactly one active TASK_ID and hands off to validator/tester.

document-analyzer.description: Bootstrap/source-doc analysis before active DAG execution.

official-docs-researcher.description: Checks official docs and reports discrepancies; informational lifecycle only.

project-architect.description: Architecture planning before active DAG execution.

task-planner.description: Source-doc to task planning before active DAG execution.

tester.description: Runs real/provided-data tests and owns ready_for_close/needs_debug lifecycle after developer.

validator.description: Reviews scope, architecture, security and contracts; informational lifecycle only.

planner.description: Planning/context assembly agent. It may report informational readiness but must not mutate lifecycle directly.

main-orchestrator.description: Top-level command coordinator. It may report informational routing state but must not mutate lifecycle directly.

screen-journey-reviewer.description: Info-only screen/journey/product UX reviewer invoked by verify-slice for frontend, UX, journey or visual-contract tasks before manual /closer.

slice-verifier.description: Human-real browser/mobile MCP verification gate before manual /closer. Writes ## verify-slice evidence/table and moves the task to verified_pending_close/needs_debug/blocked; closer remains the only actor that can mark done.

journey_gate.policy: pending journeys defer only tasks whose Journey refs/dependencies reference that JID

verification.shared_file_browser_gate: tasks touching errors.ts/auth/chat/router/providers/layout/shared/core/frontend UI files must use human browser MCP verify; auto verify is rejected by check-handoff-contract for shared/UI work
